description: curvine-fuse metrics — DevOps troubleshooting dashboard. Rows are ordered as a triage path: Overview -> Bottleneck Locator -> Instance Overview -> Operation drilldown -> Reply -> Framework -> Metadata -> Stream IO -> Cache -> State. First three rows are expanded by default. See etc/grafana/README.md for the full DevOps Triage Matrix and semantics. NOTE: all *_duration_us histograms are completion-based and can lag for stuck in-flight work; use inflight/queue gauges as leading signals under async/backpressure scenarios.

list.label: Opcode
list.name: opcode
list.description: Handled data/metadata/stream request ops only (34). Init/Interrupt are control ops (see Framework row); unsupported ops (Ioctl/Lseek/Rename2/...) see Overview Unsupported panel. Static list must be updated by hand when fuse_receiver.rs dispatch arms change.

list.label: IO type
list.name: io_type
list.description: Stream read/write IO only. flush/fsync/release lifecycle uses $lifecycle_io_type.

list.label: Path type
list.name: path_type
list.description: path_type=fallback means a fallback-capable handle, NOT a read that actually fell back to UFS.

title: Curvine FUSE Metrics

panels.title: Availability (up)
panels.description: Fleet availability counts over the selected $instance set: UP = scrape targets with up==1, DOWN = up==0. Sizes are independent of the number of instances (no per-instance tiles). DOWN>0 turns red. Per-instance detail is in the 'Per-instance overview' table below. Semantics unchanged: up==0 & business panels No data = process/scrape target unreachable (kernel_fd_health absent), NOT QPS=0; up==1 & QPS==0 = reachable but idle; up==1 & kernel_fd_health==0 = reachable but FUSE fd/session unhealthy.

panels.title: Kernel fd health
panels.description: Fleet FUSE-fd health counts over reachable instances: HEALTHY = kernel_fd_health==1, UNHEALTHY = kernel_fd_health==0 (process reachable but FUSE fd/session unhealthy). UNHEALTHY>0 turns red. NOTE: a down process (up==0) exports no kernel_fd_health, so it appears in NEITHER count — read this together with the Availability DOWN count. Sizes are independent of instance count; per-instance detail is in the 'Per-instance overview' table.

panels.title: Total QPS
panels.description: Zero-filled: a freshly started process with no requests shows 0 (idle), not No data.

panels.title: Request non-success ratio
panels.description: User-visible final failure SLI. status!=success over all requests. Both numerator and denominator zero-filled so all-success AND freshly-started (no requests) show 0, not No data.

panels.title: Recent shutdowns by reason
panels.description: increase over range. reason=completed|run_all_error|run_all_panic|term_signal|fd_watcher. No data means no shutdowns in the selected range (expected in a healthy running cluster).

panels.title: QPS by kind
panels.description: No grouped zero-fill (a bare vector(0) would add an unlabeled 0 line). Idle/no-traffic shows No data here; Total QPS carries the idle=0 signal.

panels.title: Error totals over range (0 = none)
panels.description: Zero-filled totals for the 4 error classes so 0 shows as 0 (not No data), green; >0 yellow/red. Detailed by-label top-N: FS errno here in Overview; Unsupported in the Framework row; delivery/enqueue in the Reply row.

panels.title: Top opcode p99 (µs) — with traffic
panels.description: Low-QPS ops can top p99 on a single slow request. Read p99 with QPS/count; low sample p99 is a hint not an SLA. non-success zero-filled per opcode so all-success shows 0. Next step: set $opcode to drill down.

panels.title: errno top-N (FS operation, uppercase)
panels.description: curvine_fuse_errors_total — FS operation layer, uppercase POSIX errno. No data means no events in selected range. (Unsupported top-N and delivery-failure breakdowns are in the Framework and Reply rows to keep the first screen lean.)

panels.title: Stage p99 by stage/kind/status (µs)
panels.description: stage=reply_write|meta_spawn|operation|stream_io. status kept so error/interrupted/unsupported are not blended. stage_duration_us has NO opcode label (global/by-kind, not per-op). Histograms are completion-based and can lag for stuck in-flight work; use inflight/queue gauges as leading signals under async/backpressure scenarios.

panels.title: Stage sample rate by stage/kind/status
panels.description: Confirms a stage has samples; a high p99 with zero samples is not a bottleneck.

panels.title: Stage non-success samples (range) — slow AND failing
panels.description: STAGE sample non-success count (stage_duration_us_count{status!=success}), NOT request count — stage_duration_us is a stage view, has no opcode, and not every request emits every stage. For request-level failure see Overview non-success ratio / Metadata non-success. No data means no non-success stage samples in range.

panels.title: Saturation: stream_write_queue_depth
panels.description: Writer-channel backlog: ALL WriteTasks (write/flush/complete/resize), not just FUSE_WRITE; resize can come from metadata paths.

panels.title: active_requests by kind
panels.description: Leading signal: high active_requests with flat stage p99 = concurrency accumulation / downstream wait (histogram lag).

panels.title: stream_lifecycle_inflight by io_type
panels.description: flush/fsync/release backlog.

panels.title: Per-instance overview

targets.expr: (sum by(instance)(rate(curvine_fuse_requests_total{instance=~"$instance",status!="success"}[$__rate_interval])) or on(instance) 0 * up{job=~"$job",instance=~"$instance"}) / clamp_min(sum by(instance)(rate(curvine_fuse_requests_total{instance=~"$instance"}[$__rate_interval])) or on(instance) 0 * up{job=~"$job",instance=~"$instance"},1e-9)

panels.description: All columns filtered by $instance. In All mode this localizes which instance is slow/down/backlogged. Click an instance then set $instance to drill down (data link is an optional enhancement).

panels.title: QPS ($opcode)
panels.description: Zero-filled so selecting an op with no traffic shows 0 (static $opcode is meant to allow no-traffic ops), not No data. $opcode covers 34 handled data/metadata/stream ops. Init/Interrupt -> Framework row Control requests; unsupported ops -> Framework Unsupported top-N.

panels.title: Count by status ($opcode)
panels.description: Full status set (success/error/interrupted/unsupported), not just success/error.

panels.title: Operation dispatch p99 ($opcode, metadata) (µs)
panels.description: Metadata dispatch scope. Navigation: if $opcode is Read/Write -> Stream IO row (io_type=read|write); if Flush/Fsync/Release -> lifecycle. stage_duration_us cannot be filtered by opcode.

panels.title: Reply write errors ($opcode)
panels.description: No data means no response write errors for the selected opcode in range.

panels.title: Kernel write latency p99 (µs)
panels.description: Kernel-fd write (splice) latency, observed on success and failure.

panels.title: Kernel write errors by errno
panels.description: Delivery failure in the sender (uppercase errno). No data means no events in selected range.

panels.title: Reply queue depth
panels.description: High depth + reply_write rate not rising = sender not draining / downstream write stuck.

panels.title: Reply enqueue failures by reason
panels.description: Reply-channel enqueue failed; the request never reached the sender. No data means no events in selected range.

panels.title: Response throughput / avg reply size
panels.description: Throughput in B/s; avg reply size (B) series overridden to bytes. Avg reply size denominator uses response_write_duration_us_count (actual sender writes), NOT requests_total (which includes no-reply/enqueue failures).

panels.title: Receiver loop wait incl. idle (NOT request latency) (µs)
panels.description: splice + header parse INCLUDING idle wait. Under low traffic a high p99 is normal idle, not slow requests.

panels.title: Control requests (Init/Interrupt)
panels.description: Init/Interrupt are handled control ops (in requests_total), excluded from $opcode drilldown. interrupted_total{opcode} counts the interrupted SetLkW, not FUSE_INTERRUPT.

panels.title: Kernel notify rate by code/status
panels.description: status=success|enqueue_failed|write_failed. Notify failures affect kernel invalidation.

panels.title: Notify failures
panels.description: No data means no notify failures in selected range.

panels.title: Stage p99 by stage/kind (framework overview) (µs)
panels.description: Framework horizontal overview. response_write/operation/io *_duration_us are the per-domain drilldowns (labels closer to domain). Two views of a stage are overview-vs-detail; do NOT subtract different metrics to localize latency.

panels.title: receive_errors_total by errno/action (lowercase errno)
panels.description: Splice/receive errors before decode. errno is LOWERCASE (enoent/eintr/eagain/enodev/other) — distinct from errors_total uppercase. action=continue|exit. No data means no events in selected range.

panels.title: Unsupported top-N by opcode/reason
panels.description: Only observation point for Ioctl/Lseek/Rename2/FsyncDir/... (kernel ops with no dispatch arm, excluded from $opcode drilldown). reason=unknown_opcode|unimplemented_opcode. No data means no events in selected range.

panels.title: decode_errors_total (phase=decode is TERMINAL)
panels.description: phase=parse is recurring/recoverable; phase=decode is TERMINAL (receiver died -> restart signal), not a rate to threshold.

panels.title: Metrics scrape self-observation
panels.description: Last-scrape self-observation. scrape_bytes is last-scrape semantics, not used for instance enumeration. p99 in µs, bytes series overridden to bytes.

panels.title: Metadata request non-success ratio
panels.description: User-visible metadata failure (authoritative). requests_total{kind=metadata}. Numerator and denominator both zero-filled so no-metadata-traffic (e.g. stream-only or freshly started) shows 0, not No data.

panels.title: Metadata dispatch duration (µs)
panels.description: operation_duration_us = metadata dispatch scope (incl reply enqueue, interrupted SETLKW excluded). NOT end-to-end; non-success -> use the request ratio panel. Do not subtract vs request_duration.

panels.title: Metadata op hotspots
panels.description: Which metadata op is hot/slow. non-success zero-filled per opcode. Pair with Cache & readdir row for Lookup/GetAttr/ReadDir hotspots. Set $opcode to drill down.

panels.title: Metadata errno top-N
panels.description: No data means no events in selected range.

panels.title: SETLKW wait p99 (µs)
panels.description: setlkw_wait includes reply backpressure, not pure lock wait.

panels.title: SETLKW inflight / interrupted rate
panels.description: setlkw_inflight is a saturation gauge; interrupted_total{opcode} = interrupted SetLkW (not FUSE_INTERRUPT count).

panels.title: IO latency p99 (µs)
panels.description: path_type=fallback = fallback-capable handle, NOT a read that actually fell back to UFS. Do not treat fallback latency/bytes as real UFS-fallback IO.

panels.title: IO error rate (io_requests status=error)
panels.description: Errors from io_requests_total{status=error}, not inferred from bytes. Zero-filled per (io_type,path_type) so a success-only path shows 0, not blank.

panels.title: IO size p95 (bytes)
panels.description: Filtered by $io_type and $path_type (io_size_bytes has both labels), consistent with the rest of Row 5.

panels.title: IO dispatch p99 & stream_io_inflight (by io_type)
panels.description: Companion to the comparison table for the two io_type-only metrics (no path_type). Dispatch (incl worker dispatch), not pure enqueue; io_type=read|write. dispatch p99 in µs, inflight series overridden to short.

panels.title: read/write comparison (by io_type/path_type)
panels.description: One row per (io_type,path_type) via merge transform: QPS / throughput (Bps, auto-scales to MB/s) / backend p99 / error rate. Dispatch p99 & inflight are io_type-only -> companion panel to the left. fallback = capable handle, not real UFS-fallback.

panels.title: Stream lifecycle duration p99 (µs)
panels.description: flush/fsync/release. Uses $lifecycle_io_type (NOT $io_type). No status label -> no lifecycle error rate (result mixes backend + reply-enqueue errors).

panels.title: Stream lifecycle attempts / inflight
panels.description: requests_total has no status (attempts only). inflight has io_type but NO path_type.

panels.title: user_meta_cache hit rate by cache
panels.description: Denominator excludes put (hit|miss only). Zero-filled per cache with 'or on(cache) 0*denom' so all-miss shows 0%, not No data. cache=status|list|blocks.

panels.title: node_cache hit rate by operation
panels.description: operation=lookup. Same zero-fill pattern.

panels.title: Cache invalidations top-N by cache/reason
panels.description: No data means no invalidations in selected range.

panels.title: Negative entry vs Lookup ENOENT
panels.description: errno is on errors_total (uppercase ENOENT), NOT requests_total.

panels.title: readdir entries distribution (success only)
panels.description: Only success child is observed. Per-syscall batch size, not total dir size. Errors -> readdir_duration_us{status=error}.

panels.title: State persist/restore by status (range)
panels.description: Near-zero events: increase over range, not rate. No status-grouped zero-fill (it would inject a label-less 0 series); No data means no persist/restore events in the selected range.

panels.title: State persist stage p99 (µs)
panels.description: stage=node_map|file_handles|dir_handles|mount_fds. A missing stage means an earlier stage failed (magic/version header failure leaves only mount_fds).

panels.title: state_persist_handle_count by kind
panels.description: Snapshot at persist time. kind=node_map|file_handles|dir_handles.

panels.title: Session init/shutdown (range)
panels.description: Low-freq lifecycle events, increase over range. No status-grouped zero-fill (would inject a label-less 0); No data means no init/shutdown events in the selected range.

panels.title: Namespaced gauges (per-instance, not summed across)
panels.description: Namespaced (not legacy *_num). Kept per-instance, not summed across instances.
